english: pa, Cs. rgya brgyáb-pa (rgya brgyab pa), to seal, to stamp; to seal up, búm-pa-la (bum pa la) a bottle Glr.; nám-mkai dbyíṅs-su (nam mkha'i dbyings su) (to seal up) into the heavenly regions, i.e. to cause to disappear, to hide for ever Glr.; to confirm or pledge solemnly by a sealed document; ri-rgya luṅ-rgya ₒdzúg-pa (ri rgya lung rgya 'dzug pa) 'to seal up hills and valleys', i.e. to protect the living beings inhabiting them from being harmed by huntsmen or fishermen, an annual performance of the Dalai Lama, consisting in a variety of spells and incantations; rgya γčóg-pa (rgya gcog pa) to break open a seal Cs. — Further expressions v. compounds. — 2. (Cs. rgyá-bo? (rgya bo?)) extent, width, size, rgyar dpag-tu-méd-pa (rgyar dpag tu med pa) immeasurable in extent Glr.; séms-čan-gyi rgyai tsad ni ći tsam (sems can gyi rgya'i tshad ni ci tsam) how vast must be the extent (of love) with respect to beings! Thgy.; rgyá-čan (rgya can) having extent, mkyén-rgya-čan (mkhyen rgya can) of extensive learning Mil.; rgya-čén(-po) (rgya chen (-po)) of very large size, very extensive, of a building, a pond etc.; grand, enormous, prodigious, of banquets feasts, sacrifices, assemblies; c. accus. very rich in, Schr.; in a general sense: great, stón-pa rgya-čé-ba (ston pa rgya che ba) a great master or teacher Thgy.; rgya-čén spyód-pai blá-ma (rgya chen spyod pa'i bla ma) may be rendered: a very virtuous Lama, po.; rgya-čúṅ (rgya chung) denotes the contrary of all this; rgya-čé-čúṅ (rgya che chung) v. rgya-kyón (rgya khyon) in Compounds; rgya-čér (rgya cher) adv. = rgyás-par (rgyas par) in detail, at large, at full length, e.g. to explain; rgya-čér byéd-pa (rgya cher byed pa) to extend Feer Introd. etc. p. 72; rgya-čer-ról-pa (rgya cher rol pa) Lalitavistara or Lalitavistâra, title of a biography of Buddha, translated and edited by Foucaux (a conjecture concerning the signification of the Sanskrit word v. Fouc. Rgyatcherr. II. p. XXII.; some statements relative to the Chinese translations of this work, v. ibid. p. XVI., and Was. 176; on the historical value of it v. Was. 3, 4); rgya bskyéd-pa (rgya bskyed pa) Zam., Cs. to widen, augment, enlarge, extend, rgya bskúm-pa (rgya bskum pa) to contract, to diminish the extent. Lastly, it also denotes, like dkyil-ₒkór (dkyil 'khor), a plain surface, a disk: nyi zlai rgya dkar śar (nyi zla'i rgya dkar shar) Mil. the bright disks of the sun and moon appear, cf. rgyas (rgyas) in zla-rgyás (zla rgyas); v. also the compounds. — 3. (Cs. rgyá-mo (rgya mo), perh. also rgyá-ma (rgya ma)) net; byá-rgya (bya rgya) fowling-net, nyá-rgya (nya rgya) fishing-net, ri-dags-rgya (ri dags rgya) hunting-net, — 4. for rgyá-ma (rgya ma), v. compounds. — 5. for rgyá-mo (rgya mo) beard, rgyá-čan (rgya can) having a beard, bearded C. — 6. for rgya-gár (rgya gar), rgya-gár-pa (rgya gar pa), and rgya-gár-skad (rgya gar skad). — 7. for rgya-nág (rgya nag), rgya-nág-pa (rgya nag pa), and rgya-nág-skad (rgya nag skad). — 8. for rgya-ru (rgya ru). — 9. for rgya-skyégs (rgya skyegs). — 10. erron. for brgya (brgya). Comp. and deriv. rgya-dkár (rgya dkar) 1. nyi-zlai rgya-dkár (nyi zla'i rgya dkar) v. above no. 2. extr. 2. Cs. = rgya-gár (rgya gar) India, 3. Cs. a dog with white spots on the nose. — rgya-skád (rgya skad) 1. Sanskrit language, 2. Chinese language. — rgya-skás (rgya skas) (W. *gya-śré* (*gya shre*)) a (European) staircase, cf. skás-ka (skas ka). — rgya-skégs (rgya skegs), rgya-skyégs (rgya skyegs), Ssk. लाक्षा, Williams: 'a kind of red dye, lac, obtained from an insect as well as from the resin of a particular tree'; in medical works it is mentioned as an astringent medicine; the adjectives dkar-rgyá (dkar rgya) light-red, and rgya-smúg (rgya smug) violaceous C. are derived from this word. — rgya-kúr (rgya khur) Med.? — rgya-kyi (rgya khyi) a Chinese dog. — rgya-kyón (rgya khyon) width, extent, area Pth., col. *gya-če-čuṅ* (*gya che chung*). — rgya-kri (rgya khri) C. chair. — rgya-gár (rgya gar) (the 'white extent or plain') India, rgya-gár-pa (rgya gar pa) an Indian, rgya-gar-skad (rgya gar skad) Sanskrit language. — rgya-grám (rgya gram) a figure like a cross; rdo-rje-rgya-grám (rdo rje rgya gram) shaped like a quadrifid flower; rluṅ rgya-grám żés-pa (rlung rgya gram zhes pa) Glr. seems to be = rluṅ-gi dkyil-ₒkór (rlung gi dkyil 'khor) atmosphere (connected with some phantastic association); lám-po rgya-grám (lam po rgya gram) a cross-road Sch. — rgya-čáṅ (rgya cang) a kind of girdle Lex. — rgya-čan (rgya can) v. rgya (rgya) 2 and 5. — rgya-ču-kúy-pa (rgya chu khuy pa) n. of a river in China near the Tibetan frontier (also rgya-ču-rabs-med (rgya chu rabs med)) Glr. — rgya-če (rgya che) etc. v. rgya (rgya) 2. — rgya-ṭám (rgya tam) Bhot. = ṭám-ka (tam ka), the third part of a rupee. — rgya-rtags (rgya rtags) mark, signature, stamp Sch. — rgya-tél (rgya thel) a kind of seal or stamp Cs. — rgya-mtóṅs (rgya mthongs) 1. a platform, an open pavilion on the house-top, 2. a vent-hole for smoke. — rgya-ₒdré (rgya 'dre) a quarrel Mil. nt. — rgya-nág (rgya nag) (the 'black extent') China, rgya-nág-pa (rgya nag pa), and -ma (-ma) a China-man and woman; rgyá-rnams (rgya rnams) the Chinese Glr.
